question.text: This Guidance Note does NOT permit netting of assets and liabilities except:

answer.text: Where basis adjustment is applied under cash flow hedges
For derivatives with the same counterparty

explanation.text: **The Logic:** No netting of assets/liabilities is allowed except for basis adjustment under cash flow hedges. All amounts in financial statements should be gross. Only P&L amounts for non-hedge derivatives may be presented net. **The Trap:** The netting exception is very narrow — only for cash flow hedge basis adjustments. **Cross-Exam Context:** Netting rules in accounting are tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Gross presentation provides more transparency about the true size of derivative positions.
